title: Exec preflight broke all cron jobs silently — fixed by switching to absolute paths
symptom: Subscription monitor, tax email scanner, and Dropbox keepalive cron jobs all appeared healthy (status: ok) but never executed their core commands. No errors surfaced to user.
root_cause: OpenClaw exec security preflight (introduced in a recent update) began rejecting shell commands that use 'cd && python3 ...' chaining patterns. All cron job payloads were written with 'cd ~/.openclaw/workspace && python3 scripts/...' which the preflight silently blocked.
fix: Updated all affected cron job payloads to use full absolute paths: 'python3 /Users/ericbrown/.openclaw/workspace/scripts/legacy/script.py'. No cd chaining, no shell operators.
lesson: NEVER use 'cd && command' patterns in cron payloads. Always use full absolute paths. OpenClaw exec preflight rejects complex shell invocations. After any OpenClaw update, audit all cron job commands for preflight compatibility.
prevention: Quarterly cron audit: run 'openclaw logs | grep error' to catch silent preflight rejections. Write cron commands with absolute paths from day one.
